common.errorBody: Content could not be loaded. Please try again.

onboarding.welcomeBody: Walk in the footsteps of Nisibis — millennia of Assyrian, Roman, Syriac and Islamic heritage. With a church and a mosque rising side by side, lively bazaars and local flavours, Nusaybin is ready to amaze you. Chart your own route.
onboarding.discoverBody: See key stops on the map, complete missions and track your tour progress as a percentage.
onboarding.locationBody: Use your live location to see nearby stops. Your location is only used on the map while the app is open and never leaves your device.

map.title: Map
map.locationDeniedBody: You can use the map without location. Allow access to see what's nearby.

explore.title: Explore
explore.searchPlaceholder: Search: coffee, church, mosque, bazaar, photo…
explore.emptyBody: Try changing your search or filter.

routes.title: Routes
routes.subtitle: Explore the city with ready-made routes

checklist.title: Missions
checklist.subtitle: Complete missions to advance your Nusaybin tour
checklist.resetConfirmBody: All completed missions will be removed. This cannot be undone.

settings.title: Settings
settings.aboutBody: Nisibis is a free city guide to help you explore Nusaybin. All tourist data is stored locally on your device.
settings.sourcesIntro: Content is compiled from the sources below. Images belong to Wikimedia Commons contributors; attributions are being verified.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.intro: We respect your privacy. Nisibis' privacy principles are simple:
privacy.locationOnlyInApp: Your location is only used on the map while the app is open.
privacy.noServer: Your location is never sent to a server.

story.title: The Story of Nusaybin
story.cardBody: A church and a mosque rising side by side — a mosaic thousands of years in the making.
story.intro: Nusaybin — known in antiquity as Nisibis — has lived for millennia on the fertile lands of Mesopotamia, along the Silk Road.
story.layersBody: From Assyrian to Roman, Persian to Syriac, Islamic to modern — every civilization left a layer and a story on this land.
story.mosaicTitle: A church and a mosque, side by side
story.mosaicBody: In the heart of the city, the Mor Yakup Church and the Zeynel Abidin Mosque rise just a hundred metres apart. Centuries of coexistence among different faiths form Nusaybin's most striking symbol; both were added together to the UNESCO World Heritage Tentative List in 2014.
story.schoolTitle: A cradle of learning: the School of Nisibis
story.schoolBody: Ancient Nisibis was renowned for its school of theology and science, remembered as a cradle of learning.
story.antiquityBody: The city's name appears in Assyrian records as 'Naṣibīna' as early as 901 BCE — giving Nusaybin nearly three thousand years of written history and making it one of Mesopotamia's most ancient settlements.

timeline.title: First named in Assyrian records
timeline.body: The city enters Assyrian inscriptions as the Aramean town of 'Naṣibīna' and is soon annexed by the Assyrian Empire.

timeline.title: Alexander & the Hellenistic age
timeline.body: After Alexander the Great's conquest, the Seleucids re-found the city as 'Antiochia Mygdonia'.

timeline.title: Three great sieges
timeline.body: On the Roman–Persian frontier, the Sasanian king Shapur II besieges the city three times, yet its walls hold; Nisibis is hailed as the 'Shield of the East'.

timeline.title: Church of Mor Yakup
timeline.body: The Mor Yakup baptistery — the most important structure surviving from the ancient city — is built in this period.

timeline.title: Rise of the School of Nisibis
timeline.body: The city's famous school becomes one of the foremost centres of theology and learning of its age.

timeline.title: The coming of Islam
timeline.body: The city peacefully joins the Islamic world under Caliph Umar and serves as an administrative centre.

timeline.title: The Zengid era
timeline.body: Under Nur al-Din Zengi the city is reshaped; the Zeynel Abidin complex bears the marks of this age.

timeline.title: The Ottoman era
timeline.body: Under Sultan Selim I, Nusaybin becomes part of the Ottoman Empire.

timeline.title: A district of Mardin
timeline.body: Today Nusaybin is a district of Mardin, carrying the legacy and multicultural fabric of ancient Nisibis.
